revenue_model_summary: Commission-based B2C marketplace: 12% take rate on GMV, supplemented by featured listing fees ($15/7 days) and seller premium subscriptions ($29/month).

gross_margin.note: High margin typical of asset-light marketplace — no inventory held

cac_analysis.primary_acquisition_channel: Instagram organic content + university DM outreach
cac_analysis.cac_benchmark_comparison: E-commerce CAC in MENA typically $15-40. Our blended $8.60 is achievable through community-first approach.
cac_analysis.cac_trend: Expected to rise to $12-15 as organic reach scales down — acceptable if LTV holds

ltv_analysis.churn_assumption_note: 7% monthly churn estimated for niche hobby marketplace; benchmarked against Etsy seller data and MENA e-commerce averages

ltv_cac_ratio.interpretation: Healthy — industry standard is >3x. At 10.4x, the business has strong unit economics even with CAC growth.

payback_period.interpretation: Buyer pays back acquisition cost in under 2 months. Very fast payback for a marketplace — allows reinvestment quickly.
payback_period.calculation: CAC $8.60 / monthly revenue per buyer $6.38 = 1.35 months

break_even.note: Very low break-even threshold — a strength of the no-code, low-overhead model

weak_assumptions.risk: Hobby buyers may order seasonally — actual rate could be 0.8/month
weak_assumptions.impact_if_wrong: LTV drops to $51 — LTV/CAC still 5.9x, still viable
weak_assumptions.test: Track repeat purchase rate of first 50 buyers over 60 days

weak_assumptions.risk: Without community features, churn could reach 12-15% as novelty fades
weak_assumptions.impact_if_wrong: LTV drops to $46 at 12% churn — LTV/CAC = 5.3x, still acceptable
weak_assumptions.test: Send re-engagement WhatsApp message to buyers who haven't ordered in 30 days

weak_assumptions.risk: As organic channel saturates, blended CAC could rise to $20+
weak_assumptions.impact_if_wrong: LTV/CAC drops to 4.5x at $20 CAC — still above 3x threshold
weak_assumptions.test: Track cost per acquired buyer by channel from day 1

pricing_tests.hypothesis: Sellers will accept 12% commission if the platform provides verified badge and analytics
pricing_tests.method: Onboard first 10 sellers at 8%, then raise to 12% for sellers onboarded from month 2. Compare retention.
pricing_tests.success_metric: Less than 20% seller churn after rate increase

pricing_tests.hypothesis: Sellers will pay $15 for 7-day featured placement if it generates 3x more views
pricing_tests.method: Offer 3 featured slots for free in month 1, measure view uplift, then charge $15 from month 2.
pricing_tests.success_metric: 30% of eligible sellers purchase a featured slot in month 2

pricing_tests.hypothesis: Top 20% of sellers will pay $29/month for analytics and priority support
pricing_tests.method: Identify top 5 sellers by GMV in month 2. Offer 1-month free trial, then charge.
pricing_tests.success_metric: 3 of 5 sellers convert to paid after free trial

overall_viability.viability_reasoning: Unit economics are strong even under pessimistic scenarios. LTV/CAC stays above 4x in all stress tests. Break-even at 20 buyers is achievable by month 2. The biggest risk is CAC growth as organic channels scale — manage this by tracking CAC by channel from day 1.
red_flags: If blended CAC exceeds $30, the economics require rethinking — either raise AOV target or improve retention
If monthly churn exceeds 15%, add community features earlier than planned

summary: The unit economics are healthy for a bootstrapped no-code marketplace at this stage. A blended CAC of $8.60, LTV of $89.30, and 1.35-month payback period give a 10.4x LTV/CAC ratio — well above the 3x minimum. Break-even requires only 20 active buyers, achievable by month 2. The three weakest assumptions (repeat order rate, churn, and CAC growth) all survive stress-testing with LTV/CAC staying above 4x. The most important early metric to track is monthly repeat purchase rate.
